sound_type: bird-like contact chirp -- 'PEEP' or 'CHIRP'. 1,000-3,000 Hz. 0.1-0.3 seconds. Mother-kitten communication. E=4
ecosystem: Forest. Coniferous and mixed forest. Denning areas, sheltered corridors. Close-range family communication.
season: Year-round. Kittens born any season, peak spring.

coordinates.description: Cougar in Cascades forests. Denning areas in secluded locations.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Felidae. Cougar scream/chirp comparison pair (212-213). Same species, different vocalization.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Felidae: Cougar Scream (212, E=7), Cougar Chirp (213, E=4).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Puma concolor = 'one-colored cat'. Chirp is the intimate family call.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Puma -- same species as 212, different vocalization.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Felidae: second entry. Scream/chirp pair complete.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Apex predator's gentle side.

song_metrics.note_types: Contact chirp (mother-kitten), courtship chirp (adult), distress chirp (kitten, higher pitch).
song_metrics.timbre: Bird-like, whistling, gentle. Nothing like the scream.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Intimate, close-range. Designed for family communication, not broadcast.
song_metrics.tempo: Chirps in quick succession during contact. Slower during courtship.
song_metrics.context: Mother-kitten contact. Courtship. Kitten distress. Den and corridor.
song_metrics.key_property: COUGAR CHIRP. Bird-like contact call. E=4. Second Felidae. Scream/chirp pair (212-213). v1.49.354.

pelage.overall: Tawny/golden-brown coat. Kittens: spotted for camouflage, spots fade by 6 months.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Kittens: spotted. Adults: plain tawny. Long heavy tail.

physical_description.overall: Adults: 50-100 kg. Kittens: ~1 lb at birth. 1-6 per litter (2-3 typical). Born blind.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Kittens: spotted coat, blue eyes, small. Adults: large tawny cat.
physical_description.field_id: Kittens are rarely seen -- if found, leave immediately. Mother is nearby. Do not approach.

nodes.title: Scream/Chirp Pair -- Vocal Range of Apex Predator
nodes.description: Same species, E=7 and E=4. Broadcast vs. intimate. Terror vs. tenderness. 1-3 km vs. 5-30 meters.

nodes.title: Kitten Communication -- Vulnerable Apex
nodes.description: Born blind and spotted. 40-70% first-year mortality. Each chirp: alive. 12-24 month dependency. Adulthood is silence.

nodes.title: Intimate vs. Broadcast Vocalization
nodes.description: 5-30 meters vs. 1-3 km. Same vocal apparatus, radically different social functions. Chirp for family, scream for reproduction.

cross_references.relationship: SCREAM/CHIRP PAIR! Same species, E=7 to E=4. Terror to tenderness. Broadcast to intimate.
FELIDAE CONTINUES! Cougar chirp (E=4) to bobcat yowl (E=5). Mother's whisper to smaller cat's rasp.
Same E=4. Both contact calls. Fox territorial, cougar familial. Different families, same energy.
PREDATOR PATIENCE! Motorik groove (E=6) and mother's chirp (E=4). The stalk and the nest.
